D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Wien's Displacement Law

track how a blackbody's peak wavelength slides inversely with its temperature

Differentiate the Planck curve and the peak lands at λ = b/T. Double the temperature and the peak wavelength halves — a clean inverse law spanning embers to stars.

Slide the temperature and watch the peak wavelength travel the inverse curve. Labelled markers show where familiar sources fall — incandescent bulb, Sun, blue stars.
